hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic complementarity, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are structurally suboptimal, and off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions are limiting selectivity gains. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutagenesis on key selectivity positions should improve both yield and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce structural failures, which dominate the QC gate failures.
Focusing optimization on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) will help disrupt conserved-pocket contacts and improve selectivity.
